articles.title: SEC Revokes SAB 121 Policy Allowing Banks to Custody Crypto
articles.description: The SEC announced Staff Accounting Bulletin (SAB) No. 122, canceling the previous guidance under SAB 121, which discouraged banks from holding Bitcoin in custody. This means banks and traditional financial institutions can now offer crypto services without significant regulatory hurdles. US Banks Can Custody Bitcoin SAB 121 required companies holding crypto-assets for their customers (like...

articles.description: Could the potential XRP futures launch kickoff a meteoric XRP surge to $15?

articles.title: President Trump signs an executive order for a national Bitcoin strategic reserve
articles.description: The ‘crypto president’ has delivered on yet another promise he made to the industry. In an iconic display of loyalty on Jan. 23rd, 2025, Mr. Donald Trump signed an executive order to create a national Bitcoin strategic reserve. articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump addressed the World Economic Forum via livestream on Thursday morning.

articles.title: Ethereum Aims to Position ETH as the Cryptocurrency for Wall Street, According to Bloomberg
articles.description: TL;DR Ethereum seeks to establish itself as the preferred blockchain for financial institutions on Wall Street, emphasizing its security and track record. Vitalik Buterin and the Ethereum Foundation support Etherealize, a startup aiming to market Ether within the financial sector. Solana, Ethereum’s direct competitor, has gained ground due to its speed and adoption by popular ... Read more

articles.title: Circle Launches Paymaster to Pay Gas Fees in USDC
articles.description: Circle launched a new on-chain utility that allows users to pay for gas fees with USDC on Arbitrum and Base. Developers can use Paymaster to streamline gas payments within their applications. Paymaster charges 10% of the gas fee but is free until June 30 2025. Circle announced the launch of a new permissionless service, called […] The post Circle Launches Paymaster to Pay Gas Fees in USDC appeared first on CoinJournal.

articles.title: ‘Heated’ Ethereum Foundation debate signals need for change — Joe Lubin
articles.description: The heated online debate over the Ethereum Foundation and a change in governance signals the need for a shift in direction, says the Consensys founder.

articles.title: Ethereum L2 Network Corn Secures $8.3M Token Round from Polychain, Laser Digital and Others
articles.description: The company plans to use the capital to further integrate Bitcoin into the DeFi ecosystem by using a hybrid tokenised version, BTCN as its gas token. articles.title: XRP price targets $15 amid enthusiasm over CME futures launch
articles.description: XRP’s 550% price rally since Nov. 5 has seen it break out of a symmetrical triangle, with a profit target above $15. Will a potential launch of XRP futures help?

articles.title: Cathie Wood won’t invest in Trump coin, will stick to the ‘big three’
articles.description: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, said she won’t buy Trump coin, calling it a memecoin without utility. Her focus remains on Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana.

articles.title: Ethereum Launches Etherealize to Bridge Blockchain and Finance
articles.description: Ethereum’s Etherealize aims to connect blockchain with traditional finance, driving institutional adoption amid competition and internal challenges.
